one_liner: CMG +2.94% but DCF shows 139.9% overvaluation ($14.59 base vs $35) — Chipotle's premium valuation requires perfect execution of throughput improvements and menu innovation.

price_action.intraday_story: CMG rallied from $33.96 open to close at $35 on below-average volume. Low conviction move.

news_catalyst.headline: CMG vs EAT (Chili's) value comparison driving fast-casual sector debate
news_catalyst.summary: Barron's coverage comparing CMG and EAT (Brinker/Chili's) highlights the valuation divergence in fast casual. CMG trades at a significant growth premium. The stock moved +2.94% but on low volume without a specific catalyst.

technical.summary: Low-volume move. DCF overvaluation is the concern.

wyckoff_narrative: No strong signal. Premium fast-casual restaurant trading at 2.4x DCF base.

valuation.summary: Even the DCF bull case of $27.45 is 22% below current $35. CMG trades entirely on brand premium and unit expansion optionality.

key_strengths: Unit expansion runway in international markets
key_concerns: Consumer wallet pressure reducing visit frequency
fundamentals.summary: Premium quality restaurant but extreme valuation. No entry at current levels.

risks: SSS (same-store-sales) deceleration would compress the multiple sharply
Trading above even the bull DCF scenario
